description_long: This object is a compact, cylindrical flashlight with a textured grip section and a slightly flared head housing a circular lens. The body appears to be made of black anodized metal, likely aluminum, with knurled patterns for improved handling. The head of the flashlight is wider than the tail, and the lens is recessed within a protective bezel. The tail end is flat, possibly allowing the flashlight to stand upright, and may feature a push-button switch for operation. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for handheld use in various urban environments. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional, tactical appearance.
description: A compact, black cylindrical flashlight with a textured grip and a wide, circular lens at the front. The design is robust and utilitarian, with a knurled body for secure handling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, circular lens recessed within the protective bezel.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full cylindrical body, textured grip, and the relative proportions of the head and tail.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the flat tail end, likely where the power switch is located.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the knurled grip and overall length.
